title: ericameria cuneata
intro: Ericameria cuneata, also known as Bigelow sage or Hop-sage, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, cuts, and skin infections. The health benefits of Ericameria cuneata include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
